title: toxicodendron bolusii
intro: Toxicodendron bolusii, also known as the African poison ivy or poison tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of East Africa. The plant contains urushiol, a highly allergenic oil that can cause severe skin irritation and allergic reactions in many individuals. Despite its toxic properties, Toxicodendron bolusii has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which may provide health benefits such as wound healing and pain relief. In herbal preparations, the plant's extracts are often used to treat skin conditions, fevers, and other ailments, although caution and proper handling are essential due to its highly allergenic nature.
